2026-04-09T09:12:45.0823076Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-09T09:12:45.0823502Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-09T09:12:45.0823860Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m

If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-09T09:21:15.1324592Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T09:13:53.442643022Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57990,\"title\":\"[Sql] Docs: Update CHANGELOG, README key concepts, and examples for 1.4.0 release\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #57982) by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe 1.4.0 GA release upgraded the REST API version to `2025-01-01` and updated dependent libraries (Azure.Core 1.52.0, Azure.ResourceManager 1.14.0). The release also deprecated the `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` property (marked `[Obsolete]` and `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]`) in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`, which was not documented in the CHANGELOG. The package also carries AOT compatibility (added in beta.2) and marks obsolete all APIs dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 API version.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG 1.4.0 entry is sparse:** Does not mention the deprecation of `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`, or the persistence of AOT compatibility and the 2014-04-01 API retirement warnings through to the GA release\\n- **README Examples section lacks SQL-specific samples:** The section only links to a generic `(aka.ms/redacted) page; two SQL-specific sample files exist at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/` (`Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md`, `Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md`) but are not referenced in README.md\\n- **README Key concepts section is generic:** Only links to a generic Azure SDK concepts page; it should describe SQL-specific resources managed by this library (SQL servers, databases, elastic pools, managed instances, backup policies, audit policies, encryption protectors, etc.)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `## 1.4.0 (2026-04-09)` section to add missing details:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.4.0 (2026-04-09)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Upgraded api-version to `2025-01-01`.\\n- `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` is AOT-compatible (carried forward from 1.4.0-beta.2).\\n\\n### Deprecations\\n\\n- `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Use `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` instead.\\n- All classes and methods dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 REST API version are marked obsolete. See (learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for details.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Upgraded dependent Azure.Core to 1.52.0.\\n- Upgraded dependent Azure.ResourceManager to 1.14.0.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md`\\n\\n**Key concepts section** — replace the current content with SQL-specific information:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` library lets you manage the full lifecycle of Azure SQL resources:\\n\\n- **SQL Servers** (`SqlServerResource`): Create, update, delete, and configure Azure SQL logical servers.\\n- **SQL Databases** (`SqlDatabaseResource`): Create and manage databases within a server, including geo-replicas and point-in-time restore.\\n- **Elastic Pools** (`ElasticPoolResource`): Group databases that share a set of resources.\\n- **Managed Instances** (`ManagedInstanceResource`): Create and manage Azure SQL Managed Instance environments.\\n- **Managed Databases** (`ManagedDatabaseResource`): Databases hosted within a Managed Instance.\\n- **Encryption Protectors** (`EncryptionProtectorResource`): Control Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) keys.\\n- **Audit \\u0026amp; Threat Protection Policies**: Configure server/database blob auditing, extended auditing, and Advanced Threat Protection.\\n- **Backup Policies**: Manage short-term and long-term backup retention policies.\\n- **Failover Groups** (`InstanceFailoverGroupResource`): Configure geo-failover groups for high availability.\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n**Examples section** — replace the current content to reference the existing SQL-specific sample files:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following samples demonstrate common scenarios using this library:\\n\\n- [Managing Managed Instances](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md) — Create, list, update, and delete Azure SQL Managed Instances.\\n- [Managing Managed Databases](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md) — Create, list, and delete Managed Databases within a Managed Instance.\\n\\nAdditional code samples can be found in the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet-backed examples in the README are required for this issue. The existing `Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.cs` and `Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.cs` in `tests/Samples/` already have `#region Snippet:` regions used in the `samples/` markdown files. This issue only adds direct links in `README.md` to those already-existing sample markdown files.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` already contains all six required sections. The changes above improve:\\n- **Key concepts** — from a generic one-liner to SQL-specific resource descriptions\\n- **Examples** — from a single generic link to direct links to the two existing SQL sample files plus the generic link\\n\\nNo sections need to be added; only existing content needs to be enriched.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` — confirm no README changes are introduced (snippets are already injected into `samples/` files, not into README directly)\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` — no public API surface changed; this is a documentation-only update\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; This issue only adds direct links in `README.md` to those already-existing sample markdown files.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` already contains all six required sections. only existing content needs to be enriched.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` — confirm no README changes are introduced (snippets are already injected into `samples/` files, not into README directly)\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` — no public API surface changed; this is a documentation-only update\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 227,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDY0MjI3\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30464227?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/HarveyLink\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/subscriptions\"}],\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM6NwQiU\"},{\"id\":2138760952,\"number\":42027,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"[BUG] SqlDatabaseResource.SetTagsAsync() throws System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'id')\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen calling SetTagsAsync() from an SqlDatabaseResource object, the following error is thrown:\\r\\n\\r\\n\\u003e Microsoft.AspNetCore.Diagnostics.ExceptionHandlerMiddleware: An unhandled exception has occurred while executing the request.System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'id')at Azure.Core.Argument.AssertNotNull[T](T value, String name)at Azure.ResourceManager.ArmResource..ctor(ArmClient client, ResourceIdentifier id)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource..ctor(ArmClient client, ResourceIdentifier id)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource..ctor(ArmClient client, TagResourceData data)at Azure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResourceOperationSource.Azure.Core.IOperationSource\\u003cAzure.ResourceManager.Resources.TagResource\\u003e.CreateResultAsync(Response response, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.NextLinkOperationImplementation.OperationToOperationOfT`1.UpdateStateAsync(Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationInternal`1.UpdateStatusAsync(Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.UpdateStatusAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)at Azure.Core.OperationPoller.WaitForCompletionAsync(Boolean async, OperationInternalBase operation, Nullable`1 delayHint, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\r\\n\\r\\nInterestingly, the tags are saved correctly. The error must occur after the save operation.\\r\\n\\r\\nImportant points:\\r\\n\\r\\n- The error only occurs when using a user managed identity (I host it in a Windows-based App Service).\\r\\n- When running locally using VisualStudioCredential(), no error occurs.\\r\\n- The error only occurs when Azure.ResourceManager v1.4 or later is used.\\r\\n- By default, Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 targets Azure.ResourceManager v1.3.2 - using these versions together results in no error.\\r\\n- Using Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 with Azure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 onwards causes the issue.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nI expect that I can use Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0 with Azure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 onwards, and not receive an error when saving tags to a SqlDatabaseResource when accessed via a system managed identity.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nI receive the above error.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n1. Create a .Net 6 MVC web application.\\r\\n2. Import the following NuGet package: Azure.ResourceManager.Sql v1.2.0\\r\\n3. Import the following NuGet package: Microsoft.Identity.Web v2.16.1\\r\\n4. Add an AzureSql database (I have used DTU-based free tier).\\r\\n5. Create an App Service in Azure - Windows - .NET 6 runtime.\\r\\n6. Create a custom role which has \\\"Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/read\\\" and \\\"Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases/write\\\" permissions.\\r\\n7. Create a system managed identity which applies the custom role on the database.\\r\\n8. Associate the system managed identity with the app service.\\r\\n\\r\\nAdd the following code into the home controller of the MVC project (fill in the todo parts with Azure info):\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\npublic async Task\\u003cIActionResult\\u003e Privacy()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    await SetTags();\\r\\n\\r\\n    return View();\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nprivate async Task SetTags()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    SqlDatabaseResource db = await GetDatabase();\\r\\n\\r\\n    var tags = new Dictionary\\u003cstring, string\\u003e()\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        {\\\"tag1\\\", \\\"tag1val\\\"},\\r\\n        {\\\"tag2\\\", \\\"tag2val\\\"},\\r\\n    };\\r\\n\\r\\n    await db.SetTagsAsync(tags);\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nprivate async Task\\u003cSqlDatabaseResource\\u003e GetDatabase()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    ArmClient client = GetClient();\\r\\n\\r\\n    string subscriptionId = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    string rgName = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    string serverName = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    string dbName = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n\\r\\n    ResourceIdentifier resourceId = SqlDatabaseResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, rgName, serverName, dbName);\\r\\n    SqlDatabaseResource sqlDbResource = client.GetSqlDatabaseResource(resourceId);\\r\\n\\r\\n    SqlDatabaseResource db = await sqlDbResource.GetAsync();\\r\\n\\r\\n    return db;\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nprivate ArmClient GetClient()\\r\\n{\\r\\n    if (_hostEnvironment.IsDevelopment())\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        return new ArmClient(new VisualStudioCredential());\\r\\n    }\\r\\n\\r\\n    string managedIdentityClientId = \\\"todo\\\";\\r\\n    return new ArmClient(new ManagedIdentityCredential(managedIdentityClientId));\\r\\n}\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nNow publish the project to the App Service.\\r\\n\\r\\nAt this point, running the code via Visual Studio, or via the App Service will write the tags to the DB correctly when navigating to the Privacy link (no errors).\\r\\n\\r\\nNow Add the following NuGet package:\\r\\nAzure.ResourceManager v1.4.0 (or above).\\r\\n\\r\\nPublish the application to the App Service again.\\r\\n\\r\\nThis time, an error will be observed when clicking on the Privacy link when run in the App Service.\\r\\n\\r\\n(Although it works ok when run in Visual Studio still).\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nVisual Studio for running locally.\\r\\nAzure App Service - Windows - .NET 6.0.\\r\\nAzure SQL Database - 5 DTU free DB.\\r\\nCustom Role with read and write permissions on SQL databases (details above).\\r\\nUser Managed Identity applying the custom role to the DB.\\r\\n\",\
